topic: night harvesting and grape freshness in warm climates
trigger_keywords: cosechar de noche reduce la temperatura de la uva y disminuye el riesgo de oxidación

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Grapes in a warm climate are harvested during the cooler night rather than after daytime heating.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Cooler fruit arrives at the winery with less heat-driven oxidation and volatilisation, and requires less rapid chilling before processing.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Fresh aromas and the acidity already present in the grapes are better preserved; night harvesting does not itself create additional acidity.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Fresh aromas and the acidity already present in the grapes are better preserved; night harvesting does not itself create additional acidity.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
